mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS path lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so negative month-over-month changes in policy rate predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS falls over the prior month, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recession-warning threshold signals deteriorating household income and weaker corporate demand, which should pressure equity prices lower over the next month. The signal should be more negative once unemployment is already elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce negative 20-day returns more often than positive returns, or if the average 20-day return is not below the unconditional sample mean by at least 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS relative to its recent level indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should lift near-term S&P 500 returns as analysts revise earnings expectations upward.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month RSAFS growth does not coincide with higher-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relationship flips for more than one-third of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates manufacturing and industrial output recovery, which improves cyclical earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month INDPRO uptrends are followed by negative or flat 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the average 60-day return is not materially above the sample median.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS identifies broadening labor income and retail demand, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns because the signal captures both employment and spending strength rather than either alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not statistically positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is at or near the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL is still rising, equity markets interpret policy as constrained and inflation as a margin-cost risk, so forward SPX returns should be negative over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and CPIAUCSL is rising on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate/high-inflation state is not associated with below-average or negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate is indistinguishable from 50%.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening labor market, falling UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS reflects stronger real activity and better earnings power, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if combined unemployment declines and payroll gains do not precede positive 5-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the signal loses predictive power once UNRATE is below 6%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and a spillover into broad cyclical spending, so S&P 500 returns should turn negative over the next quarter after a sizeable housing downturn.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST drawdowns are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal remains positive during repeated housing contraction episodes.
